title: The gpt-image-2 infrastructure dance
summary:
A solid day of infrastructure tuning, bringing massive capacity upgrades to gpt-image-2 and a slick new integration for OpenClaw coding agents.

We did a little routing shuffle with `gpt-image-2` today to handle load. First, we spread traffic across five Azure regions. Shortly after, we decided to drop the round-robin entirely and route directly to OpenAI. The end result is what matters: significantly higher generation capacity and fewer rate limits for your image requests.

Pollinations is now a built-in provider for OpenClaw. We spun up a dedicated `openclaw` model alias backed by Qwen3-Coder-30B and added a CLI installer. Hooking up automated coding agents with built-in tool-call sequencing just got a lot faster.

Fixed two API quirks that were causing unnecessary server panics. Sending dead silence to the Scribe audio transcription endpoint now returns an empty string instead of a 500 error. Also, requesting a text model on an image endpoint now politely returns a 400 instead of a confusing 502, which keeps our analytics cleaner and your debugging faster.
